We first calculate \(\Delta H^\circ\) and \(\Delta S^\circ\) for the reaction:

\[
\Delta H^\circ = [(-26.40) + 3(0)] - [(-17.89) + (-57.80)] = -26.40 + 75.69 = 49.29 \text{ kcal/mol}
\]

\[
\Delta S^\circ = [47.3 + 3(31.2)] - [44.5 + 45.1] = (47.3 + 93.6) - 89.6 = 140.9 - 89.6 = 51.3 \text{ cal/mol·K}
\]

Then at 298 K:

\[
\Delta G^\circ = \Delta H^\circ - T\Delta S^\circ = 49.29 - (298 \times 0.0513) = 49.29 - 15.29 = 34.0 \
